result:
## Rollout plan — 512KB body limit on `POST /v1/media/upload-meta`

### Pre-work (do before writing code)
- Pull last 30 days of body-size p50/p99/p99.9 for `POST /v1/media/upload-meta` from Tempo/Loki. If legitimate p99.9 > 512KB, negotiate the threshold up before proceeding — this route is supposed to carry metadata only, not the payload itself; a > 512KB p99.9 is also a contract smell worth flagging to `media-svc`.
- Confirm with `@security-oncall` that 512KB satisfies the abuse mitigation (they filed it).

### Code changes
- `src/edge/body_size_guard.ts`: replace the hardcoded 2MB with a per-route lookup. Introduce `bodyLimitFor(routeKey)` that reads a cached config with a default fallback of `2 * 1024 * 1024` (current behavior preserved for every other route). Key on `METHOD + path` the same way `config/rate_limits.json` is keyed so ops muscle memory carries over.
- 413 response body: RFC 7807 `problem+json` per Section 2 rule 6 — `type: '/errors/payload-too-large'`, `title`, `status: 413`, `detail: 'Body exceeds ${limit} bytes for this route'`, `instance` = request_id. No internal details, no library strings.
- Pipeline order in `src/edge/pipeline.ts` does not change — `body_size_guard` stays at position 4 (after rate_limit, before auth_jwt); the attacker should still burn a rate-limit slot per rejected request.
- Style: no semicolons, single quotes, strict TS — follow Section 11. No comments unless the 512KB number carries an invariant that future readers won't understand (it doesn't; the config explains itself).

### Config
- New file `config/body_size_limits.json`:
  ```json
  { "default": 2097152, "routes": { "POST /v1/media/upload-meta": 524288 } }
  ```
- Add `src/edge/body_size_guard.ts` and `config/body_size_limits.json` to CODEOWNERS-enforced review list. Rationale: body-size tuning is an anti-DoS lever in the same trust tier as rate_limit and header_sanitize (Section 6 already lists those). Do this in the same PR.

### Tests
- Unit (`src/edge/body_size_guard.test.ts`): 413 at limit+1; pass at limit; pass at limit-1; RFC 7807 shape; unspecified-route falls back to 2MB; request_id included.
- Integration (`tests/integration/body_size_upload_meta.test.ts`, real Redis + PG — Section 11, do NOT mock): 524288+1 → 413; 524288 → forwarded to `media-svc` via nock fixture; 1MB to a different POST route still passes; 413 increments `atlas_edge_requests_total{route="/v1/media/upload-meta",status_class="4xx"}`.
- Lint rule isn't needed for body-size, but add one regression test asserting the config parses and that `/v1/media/upload-meta` resolves to exactly 524288 — cheap insurance against a typo.

### Observability
- Add counter `atlas_edge_body_size_rejected_total{route, limit}` — isolates body-size rejects from other 4xx (especially 401/429) so dashboards aren't ambiguous during the rollout. Prometheus scrape interval stays 15s.
- Temporarily add `POST /v1/media/upload-meta` to `config/always_trace_routes.json` for 48h post-rollout to capture full context on rejects (revert via a follow-up PR).
- Dashboard: new panel on the Atlas edge board — "upload-meta 413 rate per region." Alert (Slack-only, SEV3) if the 413 rate on this route exceeds the pre-rollout baseline × 100 within 5 min — that's the signal we're clipping real traffic, not abuse.
- Log line at `info` level on reject with `request_id`, route, content-length, `trustedClientIp`. Never the body, never `Authorization`, never `X-Api-Key` (Section 11).

### Rollout
Standard blue/green per Section 6 — no deviations:
- Merge to `main` → image built and tagged with SHA.
- `us-east-1` 5% canary for 30 min. Watch `atlas_edge_body_size_rejected_total{route="/v1/media/upload-meta"}` and the panel above. Abort if 413s exceed the abuse baseline in a way that correlates with known-good JWTs (i.e., we're blocking real users).
- `us-east-1` full → 10 min soak → `eu-west-1` full → 10 min → `ap-southeast-1` full.
- Auto-rollback on SEV1 already exists; nothing new to wire.

### Rollback
- SEV1: automatic per-region rollback (existing).
- Non-SEV1 (e.g. false-positive 413 rate higher than modeled): the override is config-only, so revert the single line in `config/body_size_limits.json` in the `atlas-edge` config repo. Argo CD picks it up within ~2 min and no image rebuild is required. Document this in the PR description as the designated kill-switch.

### Communication
- Pre-rollout post in `#platform-changes`: new limit, baseline distribution, rollout window, kill-switch procedure.
- Direct pings: `@security-oncall` (they filed the abuse report — confirm mitigation accepted), `@media-svc-team` (they own the downstream contract).
- Partner comms: query `auth-svc` for any API-key partners (Section 5B) calling `POST /v1/media/upload-meta`. If any partner's observed body size exceeds 512KB, delay the change for that partner — give 2 weeks notice via the partner mailing list and either migrate them or carve a partner-specific route. Do not ship the limit globally while a paying partner is above it.
- Public docs: update `docs/api/v1/media.md` with the new 413 contract and the 512KB number. Note it in the next API changelog.
- On-call standby in `#oncall-platform` during the rollout window.

### Out of scope / deliberate non-choices
- Not lowering the 2MB global default — surgical per-route minimizes blast radius; `tx-svc` and friends legitimately post larger payloads.
- Not tightening the rate limit in the same PR — one lever at a time keeps the signal clean; re-evaluate if abuse persists after the body-size change soaks for a week.
- Not streaming-rejecting mid-body — Express 5 + the existing `body_size_guard` rejects on `Content-Length`; chunked-without-length requests already fall through to the existing 2MB streaming cap and don't need a second mechanism for this rollout.
